Ships: 1600: Tow line attatched to Golden Forest which had no steering control. Heavy rip tides in narrows. 1643: Tow line parted. 1723 new line attatched and underway on 360T 1735 vessel reported steering failure 1750 tow line parted. 1830 underway vessel in tow. Golden Forest steering gear working. 1922 Golden Forest steering gear out of order. 1745 Tow rope parted. Master of Gloden Forest sent message he would try and make way under own power. 2350 Received information from Master Golden Forest that se was setting badly at the head with forward decks awash and requested that an attempt be made to beach her in Akutan Hbr.

Note: 2202: East Tan Akun Hd abm 2mi cc 300T 1037 cc 270T
0000: Cruising as before on course 270T in convoy with SS Golden Forest. 0030 Manouvered towards Golden Forest.

Ships: 0105: Received word from Master Golden Forest that he wished to lay to until morning with stern of vessel towards swell. 0115 Received word from GF water was creep in fire room and requested boat come alongside for personal effects of crew. 0125 send moter launch to GF. made prep for lowering life boats in case of abandonment  of GF. Played searchlight on GF to determine condition. 0136 sent message to GF suggesting she stand to the southward and proceed to Lost Hbr. Masterof GF requested Haide to pilot her into Lost Hbr.

0500: Steering gear on Gf disabled. Requested being taken in tow again. 0555 began towing GF stern first towards Akutan Hbr entrance. 0830 GF began to yaw badly to port setting Heida in position dangerously close to rocks at entrance to Akutan Hbr. 0856 chopped hawser free and stood away from rocks. Gf drifted to center of entrance to harbor. received communication that GF was able to proceed under own power. 1000 GF again became unmanagable. 1020 wreck again under control and standing towards head of bay with intent to beach at end of Akutan Hbr. 1117 Lowered boats to investigate present conditions on board wreck.

Note: 1117: Anchored 1117: At head of Akutan Hbr. Brgs Stack of Whaling stn 135T. House on sand spit 85T, Cascade 27T Lowered boats to investigate present conditions on board wreck.

Ships: 1240: Boarding party returned from GF and reported she was aground with bow abt 50 ft from the beach. Part of the cargo was 110,000 gals of kerosene and 10,000 gals of gasolene all cased. There was 6496 long tons of coal aboard. Returned personal effects to crew of GF. 1530 Intercepted radio dispatch to GF that a vessel would be charted and sent to Akutan to handle vessel.

1600: Fixed position of GF as being off Lost Hbr but owing to heavy swell making into Lost Hbr Commanding officer advised the master of the GF that Akutan Hbr would be a better shelter. for his vessel. Master of GF sent message to Commanding Officer stating he would leave the selection of a place to his vessel be taken to the commanding officer of the cutter. and believed he could hold on till reaching Akutan if the swell did not increase.
